author: Acheolg
attribution_text: Woven carbon fiber fabric, photo by Acheolg, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0)
description: Full-frame close-up of a black woven carbon fibre cloth (twill weave). Sharp, evenly lit, fills the frame edge to edge - excellent material texture for a carbon-look device body. Very high resolution. Not strictly seamless but a large flat region tiles well after cropping.

author: Christine Twigg
attribution_text: Carbon fabric woven on a Dornier weaving machine (University of Manchester School of Materials), photo by Christine Twigg, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0)
description: Second carbon fibre weave close-up, shot in a weaving lab. Slight perspective falloff toward the lower-left edge but the bulk of the frame is a clean weave. Alternative/backup to carbon_fiber_weave_woven.jpg. High resolution.

author: Andrezadnik
attribution_text: Close-up of a piece of brushed aluminium, photo by Andrezadnik, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Close-up of a brushed aluminium plate with clear directional grain. The plate is shot on a plain light-grey background, so the usable brushed-metal region is the plate interior (needs cropping to the centre). Good brushed-metal grain for a knob/bezel finish.

author: Glenn McKechnie (Graibeard)
attribution_text: Close-up of knurling on a metal tool, photo by Glenn McKechnie (Graibeard), via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 2.5 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.5)
description: Tight close-up of a knurled (cross-hatch / diamond grip) metal surface filling most of the frame. Best of the two knurling shots for use as a knob-grip texture. Portrait orientation, moderate resolution (850x1352).

author: Glenn McKechnie (Graibeard)
attribution_text: Examples of knurling produced by a knurling tool, photo by Glenn McKechnie (Graibeard), via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Two knurled tool handles lying on a wood bench, showing diamond and straight knurl bands. Not full-frame (subjects sit on a background) so it is more a reference than a tileable texture; knurled_metal_closeup.jpg is the better grip texture. Resolution 1566x738.

author: W.carter
attribution_text: Black worn leather jacket detail, photo by W.carter, via Wikimedia Commons, dedicated to the public domain under CC0 1.0 (https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/)
description: Stand-in for the 'dark tempered glass / black matte plastic' category: a genuinely black, matte, fine-grained worn leather surface. Public-domain (CC0) and very high resolution (4925x3283). Reads convincingly as a dark matte device body / strap / case material; crop a flat panel between the zips for the cleanest tile. No true CC dark-glass texture was found (the Commons tempered-glass files are all SHATTERED glass).

author: Titus Tscharntke
attribution_text: Plastic surface texture, photo by Titus Tscharntke, via Wikimedia Commons, released into the public domain (PD-self). No attribution required.
description: HONEST NOTE: despite the name this is NOT a dark/black surface - it is a LIGHT GREY ribbed/corrugated fibreglass-or-PVC panel. Included as a public-domain plastic-panel option but it does NOT satisfy the 'dark/black matte plastic' request; use dark_matte_leather.jpg for the dark matte look. Low resolution (1024x768).
